# Driver Vehicle Assignment

> Assign drivers to fleet vehicles for defined periods, maintain a full assignment history, and enforce constraints preventing double-assignment and unauthorised transfers.

**Category:** Workflow · **Version:** 1.0.0 · **Tags:** fleet · vehicle · driver · assignment · history · scheduling

## What this does

Assign drivers to fleet vehicles for defined periods, maintain a full assignment history, and enforce constraints preventing double-assignment and unauthorised transfers.

Specifies 5 acceptance outcomes that any implementation must satisfy, regardless of language or framework.

## Fields

- **vehicle** *(text, required)* — Vehicle
- **driver** *(text, required)* — Driver
- **assignment_type** *(select, required)* — Assignment Type
- **start_date** *(date, required)* — Start Date
- **end_date** *(date, optional)* — End Date
- **assigned_by** *(text, optional)* — Assigned By
- **reason** *(text, optional)* — Assignment Reason
- **end_reason** *(text, optional)* — End Reason
- **actual_end_date** *(date, optional)* — Actual End Date
- **status** *(select, required)* — Status

## What must be true

- **no_double_permanent_assignment:** A vehicle cannot have two active permanent assignments simultaneously
- **no_overlapping_assignments:** A vehicle cannot have more than one active assignment per driver type at the same time (one permanent + temporary assignments are permitted if temporal ranges do not overlap)
- **end_date_after_start:** End date must be after start date when provided
- **permanent_ends_by_new:** A permanent assignment may only be ended by creating a new assignment for the same vehicle (which auto-ends the previous one)
- **end_reason_required:** Ending an assignment requires an end_reason
- **driver_must_be_active:** Driver must have Active status in the driver registry to receive a new assignment
- **start_date_not_past:** Start date cannot be in the past for new assignments (warn if creating a backdated record)
- **decommission_ends_all:** When a vehicle is decommissioned, all active assignments are automatically ended

## Success & failure scenarios

**✅ Success paths**

- **History Query** — when a vehicle or driver filter is provided, then Full assignment history for the specified vehicle or driver is returned including ended and cancelled records.
- **Assignment Ended** — when status is active; end_reason is provided, then Assignment is ended; vehicle master's assigned driver is cleared.
- **Previous Permanent Auto Ended** — when an active permanent assignment exists for the vehicle; new assignment is being created as permanent; fleet manager explicitly confirms the transition, then Previous permanent assignment is auto-ended and the new one is activated.
- **Assignment Created** — when vehicle exists and is in active status; driver exists and has Active status; no conflicting active assignment exists for the vehicle and date range; end_date is after start_date if provided, then Driver is assigned to the vehicle; vehicle master record reflects the new driver.

**❌ Failure paths**

- **Assignment Conflict Rejected** — when an active permanent assignment already exists for this vehicle; new assignment type is permanent, then New assignment is rejected; fleet manager must end the existing assignment first. *(error: `ASSIGNMENT_CONFLICT`)*

## Errors it can return

- `ASSIGNMENT_CONFLICT` — This vehicle already has an active permanent assignment. End the existing assignment before creating a new one.
- `ASSIGNMENT_INACTIVE_DRIVER` — The selected driver is not active. Only active drivers can be assigned to vehicles.
- `ASSIGNMENT_INVALID_DATE` — The assignment end date must be after the start date.

## Connects to

- **vehicle-master-data** *(required)* — Vehicle master stores the currently assigned driver and must be updated on each assignment change
- **vehicle-expense-tracking** *(optional)* — Costs can be attributed to the driver assigned during a given period via assignment history
